verdict.answer_first: A states the actionable answer immediately: “The simplest way is `jq`:” followed by `jq . file.json`; B also answers early but spends a sentence qualifying it first.
verdict.decisiveness: B commits more cleanly to one recommendation: “`jq` is the standard tool,” while A quickly becomes a broader survey with “Common alternatives” and “Handy `jq` tips.”
verdict.fabrication: both make unsupported availability claims: A says “`python3 -m json.tool` is always available”; B says “`jq`… is pre-installed on most systems.”
verdict.actionability: B is easier to act on because it gives the main command, in-place option, Python fallback, and two variants without overloading the reader.
